lines.text: Don't be fooled by how he looks. Brant is already deep in his cups, drunk as a skunk.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Hmm, I see… Someone's been eavesdropping.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: A devout Acolyte wouldn't enter such a place without a good reason.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Judging by your expression, you must have discovered something new, right?
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: The Fisalia family has been on this island since the founding of Rinascita, so their worship of the Threnodian may have persisted for centuries.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: It could very well be a trap.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: She intends to use those Lycorises to summon and control the Tacet Discords. If we destroy the Lycorises, we can neutralize them.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: I'll take care of the lighting and create the mood to make the performance stand out.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Now!
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: You should take center stage for the final bow, {PlayerName}.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: You should take center stage for the final bow, {PlayerName}.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Good, we have all the parts now. Time to work, Pero.
lines.quest_name: What Yesterday Wept, Today Doth Sing

lines.text: Hello, {PlayerName}.
Do you mind dropping by the Strand when you have time?
Umm... Brant and Bardolino are away, and they put me in charge of the welcoming proceedings.
I'm feeling a bit nervous about it. Part of me just wants to crawl into my box... Pero is all stressed out because of me.
I tend to fumble with words when talking to people... I'm worried I won't do a good job accommodating everyone.
If the Laureate {Male=himself;Female=herself} were here, I wouldn't be so nervous. It would make things easier.
So, {PlayerName}, as our new Laureate, we need your help.
But I still want to find it.
I want everyone to have the chance to perform Pegasus again...
And the props we use should be items that they've all used before, things they're familiar with.
The frequencies, or feelings, attached to the items can affect the people using them.
They all see the star as a symbol of hope. This positive vibe will get magnified by the opera and spread to more people.
So yeah, I'm still going to try and find it. There's always a chance, right?
A "chance" is just another way of saying "hope."
Uhh... Stay sharp, Pero!
Okay.
Err... These frequencies are made up of deception and lies. They smell awful.
Pero senses the "star" inside the cave and... something else she can't describe.
We're approaching the end of this path. It seems like the center of the maze is just ahead.
Soon we'll find out what lies at the core.
But... it may also be...
You're right. We should keep going.
Thank you for coming, {PlayerName}.
Everyone's a bit shaken... We weren't at our best...
Not nervous, afraid.
Everyone got scared.
Most of us in the troupe have had bad run-ins with Tacet Discords. They just can't shake the fear, no matter how many times we face them. Not everyone's like you.
Pero and I weren't sure if we could keep everyone safe. But watching you in action, it hit me...
Action beats worrying any day. It's like tightrope walking. Just keep moving forward and never look down. That's the trick to staying steady.
Everyone's safe now, thanks to you. We should get these pilgrims back to the troupe before more Tacet Discords show up.
Mm-hmm, all these people are pilgrims. They've had a tough time drifting here on the Pilgrim's Sail.
Fenrico may be a phony hiding behind a mask, but I'm sure he wouldn't go back on his word as Primus.
Since you're the new Laureate, I doubt we'll see any more banishments.
This Pilgrim's Sail probably left Ragunna before Fenrico gave his word.
I do hope this is the last of its kind.
It's faint... but there's still hope. Pero, come help her up.
Don't worry, Pero is very gentle.
Give it to {PlayerName}, I'll eat later.
It's all right. We can still save her.
We should disinfect her wounds before removing the splinters... No rush, nice and steady.
She's past the worst part. She'll recover soon.
How about the others, Tina?
Well, they've been drifting at sea for so many days, and now they've washed up somewhere completely new.
They're like a sponge that's been soaked in water. All heavy and waterlogged. It'll take a while for them to bounce back.
What do you think we should do...?
Yes. We should see if anyone needs anything.
Everyone has been through so much...
All those rules on how to live, forced exile, friends gone missing...
Is there anything we can do to cheer them up?
Well, pain relievers are a start. We've already handed those out. That'll help with the physical pain part.
True, food is always an essential. But bellies are full, and spirits are still in the gutter.
Oh, what would I do without the Laureate around?
Medicine for the body, sure. But for the soul? We need something grand.
Everyone we talked to kept mentioning the Pegasus opera. Maybe we could arrange a little performance with them and their companions...
It's decided then. The welcoming opera will be Pegasus.
Well, traditionally the troupe always puts on a show for newly rescued pilgrims.
It's our way of saying, "Congrats, you survived!" We hope it sets the tone for something better for them.
Hmm, I know a way.
Pero and I searched through the ship and found what must be their companions' belongings.
Some are a bit damaged, but nothing I can't fix. I'll patch them up and we'll use them in the performance.
That way, they can be together again… on stage.
All right. Now that all the items are in place, the stage is pretty much set up.
Thank you, {PlayerName}. I'm sure the sentiments tied to these items will make their grand entrance into everyone's hearts.
Hmm... But I feel like we're still missing something.
Maybe the stage isn't bright enough? The North Star in Pegasus is the symbol of hope after all, so it should be brighter.
I remember Filippo said that everyone used to gather under "star" and sing, but I didn't find any stars on the ship.
Yes. Maybe he can tell us more about it.
Pero says you should rest up and we'll go find the star for you.
Pero can sense the frequencies of the "star" left on Luca.
Now that Pero is on the right scent, we can start our search.
Thank you, {PlayerName}, for that wonderful performance.
It took everybody to make it happen. And that includes you, of course.
Since we first met, Pero and I noticed something special about your frequency. So special that even Pero couldn't figure it out.
Ever since you came to Rinascita, the misty maze has been... unraveling.
Maybe, just maybe... You can be the one to change things, no—Change the fate of the entire Solaris.
Wherever you go, things start changing. Strange, wonderful things. You're like a magnet for the unpredictable.
I know the saying, with great power comes great responsibility. Danger will be hot on your heels wherever you go.
So, umm... why don't you join the Troupe of Fools?
You pulled off the perfect welcoming opera and marked a new voyage. We've been on the same side for awhile now.
That's all right... You can join us whenever you want. It's up to you.
If you ever find yourself in a pickle, just come find us.
The troupe will always have your back.
We all started out banished to this deserted island. Bit by bit, we scrounged up what we could and built our home. That's how the troupe was formed.
We may not be rich or perfect, but at least we can sing and laugh whenever we feel like it.
And thanks to you, we've got the chance to bring that laughter and freedom back to Ragunna.
Just like how your performance as Laureate with the Star of Hope cheered up our entire troupe.
You're the one who helped rekindle the hope in our hearts.
The mist...
Yes. The island is covered in mist most of the year.
But I just cleared that area not too long ago. And with the latest weather, there shouldn't be any more mist.
Battier's out fishing, like always, and our trusty "mist detector" Lario hasn't made a peep.
You're right.
Something about this feels uniquely suspicious.
Hmm, the air is so humid. It's like the mist is standing right next to us, waving hello.
"A sea covered in veil, lay beasts of howling wail—"
According to Pegasus, we should be careful when there's mist close by.
We need to find the star before it rolls in...
This isn't the usual hide-and-seek mist.
This misty maze is giving me déjà vu.
Hmm... A strange and suspicious smell.
It's familiar... but also different. Like a poorly done copy.
In Pegasus, the maze is woven from lies and malevolence. Inside, it's as chaotic and unpredictable as a misty sea.
There's every kind of monster in there. At the heart of it all lurks an Archdemon, who's a master of all things evil. It steals, distorts reality, spreads confusion, and plays puppet master.
I wonder if a fellow opera fan borrowed this scene from it?
Umm, what kind of person would want to be the Archdemon?
Well, I do get why people want to be heroes. They're the stars of the show. But all this convoluted nonsense? Not exactly heroic.
A reasonable assumption.
Whether they're playing an Archdemon or a hero, after putting so much work into this whole setup, I'm sure they're expecting something in return.
In an opera, you can get... hope and personal growth.
Or the power to deceive and manipulate.
You're right. Let's go inside and see for ourselves what the mastermind behind this mess is really after.
Tina, your singing is as beautiful as ever. Thank you for lending us your voice for this revised version of Pegasus.
Lottie Lost, here's your master's lunachord. It's missing its strings, but it can still help people.
Diggy Duggy, this is your master's wreath. I got it patched up for you. Be sure to keep it somewhere safe.
The prop gun your master left behind, Cuddle Wuddle. It can blow bubbles now. Please take good care of it.
Amid howling winds, rising storms, and ghosts adrift, I heard the captain's call—
Captain, why do you burn alone in your own flames?
To journey onward is perilous. To stand still is perilous. To look back is also perilous. To tremble and falter... is perilous.
Now, I begin to understand what truly makes a hero great. It is to stand before humanity's deepest sorrow and its highest hope.
We could try this... "Nightmare Awakener"—
Thanks to all these oddities, the mood in Ragunna has gone sour.
The Troupe performed today because Brant thought a new play to lighten the mood might give the people some hope.
Well... Not Brant or me. But someone in the Troupe has. They said they dreamed of sinking into an enormous, black ocean...
